items.title: Outback band launches debut EP inspired by language and country
items.summary: A group of young musicians from Queensland's Gulf country are turning stories and language into song, and are now set mentor other aspiring performers.

items.title: Health authorities investigating source of mysterious eye infections in Darwin football players
items.summary: Aussie Rules players in Darwin with symptoms of conjunctivitis are being urged to contact health authorities amid an outbreak of unknown origin affecting dozens of players.

items.title: NT parliament increases minimum sentence for domestic violence murder, speaker berates backbencher
items.summary: Northern Territory parliament has passed legislation increasing the mandatory minimum prison sentence for domestic violence murder from 20 to 25 years. During sittings this week, the speaker also berated a government backbencher over his handling of bullying claims.

items.title: Police allege newspaper boss Antony Catalano attacked woman with clothes iron
items.summary: The co-owner of national newspaper company ACM has been stood down from his position after appearing in court, with police alleging he assaulted a woman and swung a clothes iron at her head.

items.title: Wayne Bennett calls pitch invasion after Alex Johnston's NRL try-scoring record 'pretty disruptive'
items.summary: A grumpy Wayne Bennett has told reporters he has already answered enough questions about Alex Johnston's try-scoring record, and described fans rushing the field as "pretty disruptive".

items.title: Couran Cove resident dismayed as court upholds $22m body corp debt
items.summary: A resident of the Couran Cove Island Resort in the northern Gold Coast says she is shocked by a court decision upholding a $22 million body corporate debt.

items.title: Confusion and anger as Bluesfest ticketholders question timing of cancellation
items.summary: Festival revellers, who have spent thousands on the event, are angry and have been left wondering when organisers knew it was not viable.

items.title: NT floods captured in photos and video from Katherine, Beswick, Palumpa, Daly River, Darwin River
items.summary: Many regions across the Northern Territory were submerged by flooding this week. Here is some of the most dramatic footage from the extreme weather event, right across the Top End.

items.title: Tasmanian wetland rebounds as invasive rice grass nears eradication
items.summary: An invasive weed planted decades ago quickly spread across Tasmania's north-west, choking mudflats and wildlife habitat. Now, those involved in a long-running effort to wipe it out are seeing real results.

items.title: What does Katy Perry's loss in the High Court mean for her business?
items.summary: Australian fashion designer Katie Perry has won her long-running trademark battle against pop star Katy Perry, but questions about whether the singer will be able to sell her clothing merchandise as part of any future tours remain.

items.title: Melbourne would never have fallen for Kyle and Jackie O
items.summary: You could argue Melbourne didn't warm to the the duo's shock-jock style but I think the answer is much simpler.

items.title: Dennis Richardson says sense of urgency 'didn't quite align' with royal commission
items.summary: Dennis Richardson calls for a second interim report from the royal commission into antisemitism, warning it would be "unacceptable" to leave findings about any intelligence failures that contributed to the Bondi terrorist attack until the end of the year.

items.title: Everyday items that may be affected by the fuel price due to war in the Middle East

items.title: First known image of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, Peter Mandelson and Jeffrey Epstein released

items.title: An email from the wrong Stephen King was sent to journalists. This is the human impact of the bungle.

items.title: The latest injectable peptides trend on social media is worrying health experts
items.summary: The latest wellness craze to take social media by storm is fuelling a growing grey market of injectable peptides in Australia, and it has experts worried.

items.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth questions new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's ability to lead after nearly two weeks of US and Israeli attacks on Iran, saying he is "wounded and likely disfigured".

items.title: Person found dead in house fire at Morphettville
items.summary: The house on Dalby Avenue at Morphettville was fully alight when emergency services were called just after 4:30pm.

items.summary: A ship that was found to be carrying nearly 5 tonnes of cocaine near French Polynesia earlier this year has been escorted into Sydney Harbour after issuing a distress call.

items.summary: An inquiry into the potential split of a southern New South Wales health district has heard that hospitals have been unable to hire staff to fill critical gaps.

items.summary: Supreme Court judge rules convictions should have been recorded against a teenager who entered a Mackay home and molested a girl in her bedroom.

items.title: Queensland man charged over alleged antisemitic online threats
items.summary: The Australian Federal Police have charged a man over a series of alleged antisemitic online threats.

items.summary: The lead investigator in the trial of two Melbourne men over the 1999 murder of Canberra grandmother Irma Palasics tells a court his first major suspects were Mrs Palasics's family members.

items.title: Stood down senior project manager-turned-whistleblower condemns mould-ridden hospital
items.summary: Former staff blow the whistle on horror conditions at a NSW hospital, including mouldy vents, inadequate separation of sewage and drinking water, and the use of sanitary pads to soak up rainwater leaking into wards.

items.title: Only service station in NSW town of Batlow runs out of fuel amid surging prices

items.title: 'Get me out of here': Johnston says fans on the field was fun until it wasn't
items.summary: Alex Johnston becomes the greatest try-scorer in Australian rugby league history with a double against the Roosters, prompting fans to ignore the NRL's pleas not to storm the field to celebrate.

items.title: Ruling rendering NSW parliament powerless to compel witnesses used for first time
items.summary: James O'Brien from Honeywell, which is subcontracted to manage facilities at Newcastle's Calvary Mater Hospital, failed to turn up to an inquiry.

items.title: Matt Wright claimed explosive secret recordings may have been obtained illegally
items.summary: Former Netflix star Matt Wright has been ordered to pay the Northern Territory Police Commissioner's court costs after claiming a warrant to bug his home "might have been a forgery".

items.title: How young Australian actors are making a career in a tough industry
items.summary: Young actors dream of careers on stage or screen but they know finding full-time work is a tough gig, despite plenty of famous Australians making it in Hollywood.

items.title: A bruising week for Labor after an email blunder, but the blowtorch is back on the Liberals after a candidate's comments
items.summary: With two weeks before polls, Labor were in damage control after a health email blunder. It should have been a chance for the Liberals to get ahead, but after controversial comments by a candidate it's the one left bruised.

items.title: Hawthorn forward Dylan Moore allegedly found with bag of 'crystalline substance' after US arrest, police documents claim
items.summary: Hawthorn forward Dylan Moore was allegedly found with a bag of a "crystalline substance" and a rolled up $100 dollar bill when he and teammate Connor Macdonald were arrested last year, Arizona police documents claim.

items.title: WA bikie law change leaves police powerless to stop convicted child sex offenders from associating
items.summary: Changes in Western Australia's anti-consorting laws have had the unintended consequence of making it easier for convicted child sex offenders to associate with each other.

items.title: Fraser Coast childcare centre fined $15,000 over nap time death of child with multiple illnesses
items.summary: The two-year-old boy, who was suffering from COVID and other illnesses, was found unresponsive at a Queensland childcare centre in 2022.

items.title: Fuel panic is spreading and now Australia is tapping into its emergency fuel supplies
items.summary: The country's stockpile of fuel is about to be opened but experts are warning it won't solve the problem of soaring prices or shortage fears.

items.title: DV attacker took photo of victim giving evidence in contempt of court jailed for more than four years
items.summary: A man who was found guilty of domestic violence offences and who pleaded guilty to contempt of court for photographing the victim giving evidence has been jailed for more than four years.

items.title: Iranian footballers backed by Afghan international cricketer Firooza Amiri to adjust to life in Australia
items.summary: Having fled Afghanistan as an 18-year-old, international cricketer Firooza Amiri knows what it is like to be a sports refugee from a war-torn country.

items.title: Sydney businessman Alexander Csergo found guilty of reckless foreign interference
items.summary: Australian businessman Alexander Csergo is found guilty of reckless foreign interference, after a Sydney court hears he compiled reports for two suspected Chinese spies while overseas.

items.title: After almost 130 years in England, an Aboriginal man has been reburied on Country
items.summary: After almost 130 years, the remains of an Aboriginal man have made the long journey home from Oxford University to be reburied on the Country from where he was taken at Berowra Creek in Sydney.

items.title: How are rising fuel costs and oil supply shortages affecting you or your business?
items.summary: As fuel costs soar and the global economy reacts to a shortage of oil supplies, we need your help in identifying the impacts here in Australia. Whether it's the cost of your work commute or supply shortages affecting your business, share it with us.

items.title: Western Australia the big GST winner again, despite massive budget surpluses
items.summary: WA will get an extra $6.6 billion from taxpayers in the coming financial year under its special GST deal, despite being the only state with a budget surplus.

items.title: Illegal tobacco retailer's $1.5 million fine reduced to $250,000 after legal appeal
items.summary: The Emerald District Court ruled the initial fine – far beyond what prosecutors had even sought – was excessive and errors occurred in sentencing.

items.title: Parents in Alice Springs worry Birth and Beyond will close due to funding shortfall
items.summary: An "incredible" organisation delivering pregnancy and postnatal support in Alice Springs is calling for funding from both Northern Territory and federal governments to keep their doors open.

items.title: Byron Bay's famous Bluesfest cancelled with less than three weeks' notice
items.summary: Thousands of music fans will be left scrambling to rearrange their Easter plans after one of Australia's last major festivals was called off with less than three weeks' notice.

items.title: Interruptions, campaign woes and 'razzle-dazzle': Here's how the SA leaders debate played out
items.summary: As South Australians get ready to vote in a week's time, Premier Peter Malinauskas and Opposition Leader Ashton Hurn went head-to-head in a campaign debate. Here's what they said.

items.title: Qantas COVID pandemic flight credits could be on their way to customers this year
items.summary: Qantas agrees to settle a class action ​related to flight credits issued during the COVID-19 ‌pandemic.

items.title: India continues to strengthen during ICC boss Jay Shah's tenure, but it may be coming at cricket's cost
items.summary: India releases a video of its celebrations following the T20 World Cup, and it features an unhealthy amount of a man who should not be there.

items.title: Australia releases six days' worth of petrol and five of diesel from emergency stockpile
items.summary: Australia will make available about six days' worth of petrol from its emergency stockpile and five days of diesel, the first use of its fuel reserves since the invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

items.title: Jonah Pezet wants more after helping Parramatta Eels to upset NRL win over Brisbane Broncos
items.summary: Parramatta playmaker Jonah Pezet destroyed the team he will play for next year — but in a sign of his appetite for excellence, he was far from satisfied.
